name: get_tweet
description: Retrieve a single tweet by its ID. Use the 'tweet.fields' and 'expansions' parameters to control which data is returned, including public metrics (likes, retweets, replies), author details, and media attachments.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique ID of the tweet to retrieve. This is a path parameter.

parameters.name: tweet.fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tweet fields to return. Options include: created_at, author_id, public_metrics, source, lang, entities, attachments, conversation_id.

parameters.name: expansions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of expansions. Options include: author_id, attachments.media_keys, referenced_tweets.id.

parameters.name: user.fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of user fields to include when author_id is expanded. Options include: name, username, profile_image_url, verified, description.

data.text: Hello world! This is a sample tweet.
